message:
fix(doctor,tui): archive-existence check before repair reconstruct, symlink-safe atomic TUI export

Doctor repair archive-existence check (cli/lib.rs):
- doctor repair now checks whether an archive directory actually exists
  via path_is_real_directory before falling back to archive
  reconstruction, which would fail confusingly with no archive present
- If no archive exists, attempts in-place recovery from .bak sibling
  backups instead
- Two new tests: recovery succeeding from sibling backup, and failing
  closed with clear error when neither archive nor backup exists

TUI export symlink hardening (server/tui_app.rs):
- New path_existing_prefix_has_symlink() walks each path component
  checking for symlinks via symlink_metadata
- New validate_export_path() rejects any path containing symlinks
- New write_export_file_atomic() uses temp-file + rename with symlink
  re-validation before the final rename
- export_snapshot_to_dir validates the export directory before use and
  writes atomically
- Three Unix-only tests verify rejection of symlinked export
  directories, symlinked parent directories, and symlinked target files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
